text: Hard rule: use the newest stable Go for any Go service in the tatara platform. Pin the Go directive to the exact minor version in go.mod.
Hard rule KISS: prefer simplicity over cleverness. Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.
Hard rule boy-scout: if you see something easy to fix alongside current work, fix it. Do not ask first.
Hard rule: never introduce tech-debt. If a thing is complex, call it out in MEMORY.md with the rationale. Never defer cleanup to later.
Hard rule: charts are created via helm create then edited. Never hand-rolled.

Hard rule: no plain ENVs in values.yaml and no lists in values.yaml. All inputs map a camelCase scalar in values.yaml to a kebab-case key in ConfigMap or Secret, consumed via envFrom. Genuinely list-shaped data is rendered into a templated ConfigMap and read at runtime.
Hard rule: Sonnet for implementation, Opus for merges. Implementation subagents are claude-sonnet-4-6. The merge subagent that integrates parallel work is opus. Plan and review work runs in opus.
Hard rule: JSON logs only. Use stdlib log/slog in Go with the same logger structure everywhere.
Hard rule: log every business action at INFO with structured fields request_id, user, action, resource_id, duration_ms where relevant. WARN and ERROR used appropriately.
Hard rule: metrics for everything that counts, times out, or can fail. Counters for events, histograms for durations, gauges for in-flight. Expose a /metrics Prometheus endpoint on every service.

Hard rule: never kubectl set image, kubectl edit, or kubectl patch spec fields on a helm-managed resource. Bump chart appVersion and helm upgrade instead. Direct kubectl mutations leave orphan field-managers that block helm 4 server-side apply on the next sync. This burned the v0.1.1 to v0.1.2 tatara-memory upgrade.
Hard rule branch flow: worktree off main, develop in the worktree, merge back to source repo main, cleanup the worktree, then build and deploy from main only. Never build or deploy from a worktree.

Decision 2026-06-23 (issue #48): the scored /queries:data path populates QueryMatch.Score. lightrag v1.4.16 /query and /query/data expose no per-match relevance or similarity field (data.chunks carry content, file_path, chunk_id, reference_id only; relationships.weight is a graph edge weight, not retrieval rank), so Score is derived from retrieval rank as 1/(rank+1). This gives the eval a deterministic ranking signal that follows lightrag chunk retrieval order instead of reference arrival order.

Decision 2026-06-06: the deterministic structural code graph (code_entities and code_edges, jsonb props) lives in tatara-memory's Postgres; semantic text chunks stay in LightRAG. POST /code-graph:bulk is synchronous returning 200 plus counts, deliberately not the async ingest_jobs path. Re-ingest replaces at file granularity.

Decision 2026-06-06: migrations are applied at startup in run() via app.migrate (ingest plus codegraph). Previously ingest.Migrate was never called in prod. migrate() lives in run(), not newAppWithDeps, to keep app construction side-effect-free.
Decision 2026-05-27: the Makefile VERSION strips the leading v from git describe --tags. Chart appVersion and image tag now match without manual re-tagging.
Decision 2026-05-24: golangci-lint pinned to v2.11, not v1.62. v1.62 built with go1.23 cannot process go1.25 modules.

Finding 2026-06-08 (0.2.1): /code-graph:bulk Push rejected fileless entities. A go_package entity legitimately has no single owning file (FilePath empty). Fix: skip the membership check for empty FilePath on entities; edge and symbol SrcFile checks stay strict.

Finding 2026-06-16 audit-r2: empty-FilePath entities (repo or package scoped like go_package) are not reclaimed by file-granular reconcile. Reconcile deletes only via DELETE WHERE file_path per touched file. Renamed or removed repo-scoped entities orphan forever; an intentional trade-off.

Issue #30 2026-06-14: instrumented the ingest worker Pool which had zero Prometheus metrics. New internal/ingest/metrics.go adds ingest_items_total, ingest_item_duration_seconds, ingest_jobs_total, ingest_items_in_flight, and ingest_notify_dropped_total. Wired via a WithMetrics(reg) option.
Decision 2026-06-06: adopted the v0.2.0 consolidation branch with tombstone deletes and opaque Edge.ID into main. All three migrations (ingest, memory, codegraph) run in app.migrate in run().
Fix #44: treat lightrag duplicated and partial_success insert statuses as idempotent success so retries do not fail a bulk ingest.

The tatara platform is split into eight independent GitHub repositories under szymonrychu: tatara, tatara-memory, tatara-cli, tatara-memory-repo-ingester, tatara-claude-code-wrapper, tatara-operator, tatara-chat, tatara-helmfile. Not a monorepo; each component has its own CI, helm chart, Dockerfile, MEMORY.md and ROADMAP.md.

Memory recall is the platform's core value loop: every autonomous agent turn calls the query and describe MCP tools in tatara-cli, which hit tatara-memory's POST /queries and POST /queries:describe endpoints. If retrieval returns the wrong memories, every downstream agent decision degrades silently.
tatara-memory clamps the top_k field on query requests: defaultTopK is 10 when omitted, maxTopK is 500 to prevent unbounded LightRAG retrievals. A negative top_k is rejected with 400.
